message:
fix(storage): route fresh-DB init through run_v0_init_in_transaction so all migrations apply (ft-7tq4z)

`initialize_schema` had two paths for `current == 0`:

  1. `current == 0 && needs_init` (no `panes` table) → run SCHEMA_SQL,
     stamp `user_version = SCHEMA_VERSION`, record ONE audit row
     "Initial schema", return.
  2. `current == 0 && !needs_init` (tables present, no version stamp)
     → `run_v0_init_in_transaction` → repair → SCHEMA_SQL →
     `run_migrations(0)` → ensure_ft_meta.

Path (1) was the bug. SCHEMA_SQL was last regenerated before v24 added
the `policy_denied_audit` table, so a fresh DB ended up at
`user_version = 24` (per SCHEMA_VERSION) with the v24 table NEVER
created. The discrepancy was masked by everyday MCP policy-deny audit
writes failing best-effort and warn-logging silently.

The fix: collapse both branches into the same path. The truly-fresh
case is just the existing-but-unversioned case minus pre-existing
tables, and `repair_existing_v0_tables_before_schema_sql` already
gates each repair on `table_exists(...)` — it's a no-op on a
genuinely empty DB. Routing fresh DBs through the same
`run_v0_init_in_transaction` eliminates the drift permanently and
keeps the BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT atomicity guarantee from
ft-k542h / c06b230a.

`needs_init` is still consulted by other callers (status reporters,
diagnostics) — no removal, only the discriminator at the top of
`initialize_schema` is dropped.

Test changes:

* NEW: `fresh_db_init_creates_policy_denied_audit_via_v24_migration`
  pins the regression. After `initialize_schema(&conn)` on a fresh
  in-memory DB: (i) `user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`, (ii) the
  `policy_denied_audit` table exists, (iii) every documented column
  is present, (iv) a real `record_policy_denial_audit_sync` insert
  succeeds and returns row_id > 0. Any of those failing is the bug
  recurring.

* UPDATED: `schema_version_audit_trail_recorded` previously asserted
  `count == 1` and `description == "Initial schema"` — both artifacts
  of the buggy fast path. The new invariant: `count >= 1`, every
  recorded version is in `1..=SCHEMA_VERSION` with non-null
  applied_at and non-empty description, and `max(version) ==
  SCHEMA_VERSION`.

* UPDATED: `idempotent_init_preserves_version` previously asserted
  `count == 1` after two inits. The new invariant compares snapshots:
  `count_after_first > 0` and `count_after_first == count_after_second`
  (re-init must not grow the audit trail). Hard-coding "1" was
  fragile — the count now grows whenever MIGRATIONS gets a new entry.

* CLEANUP: ft-cro2u's
  `wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached`
  no longer needs the v24 CREATE TABLE pre-seed; the workaround block
  is deleted with a one-line comment pointing at this fix.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features \
      --lib storage::tests::

  + cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features mcp,asupersync-runtime \
      --lib mcp_tools::tests::wait_for_tool_persists

188 storage::tests pass (3 unrelated recorder_storage frankensqlite
gate failures pre-exist this commit; not in scope). The ft-cro2u
audit-persist test passes without its workaround.

files.patch:
@@ -8012,33 +8012,10 @@ exit 17",
         runtime.block_on(async {
             let (_dir, db_path) = temp_db_path();
 
-            // SCHEMA_SQL drift workaround: storage.rs:4440-4446 takes the
-            // fresh-DB fast path (execute SCHEMA_SQL → set user_version =
-            // SCHEMA_VERSION) and never runs the per-version migrations,
-            // so a new DB skips the v24 `policy_denied_audit` table that
-            // landed via migration. Existing-user DBs hit run_migrations()
-            // and DO get the table; brand-new test DBs don't. Open a
-            // direct rusqlite connection here and apply the v24 CREATE
-            // TABLE up-front so the persist path has a target. See the
-            // follow-up bead for the SCHEMA_SQL fix.
-            {
-                let conn = rusqlite::Connection::open(db_path.as_path())
-                    .expect("open db for v24 table seed");
-                conn.execute_batch(
-                    "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS policy_denied_audit (
-                        id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
-                        ts_ms        INTEGER NOT NULL,
-                        agent_id     TEXT,
-                        tool_name    TEXT NOT NULL,
-                        intent_hash  TEXT,
-                        reason       TEXT NOT NULL,
-                        reason_code  TEXT NOT NULL,
-                        rule_id      TEXT,
-                        decision     TEXT NOT NULL
-                    );",
-                )
-                .expect("seed v24 policy_denied_audit table");
-            }
+            // ft-7tq4z is fixed: fresh DBs now run migrations, so the
+            // tool's StorageHandle::new opens with the v24
+            // policy_denied_audit table already created. No workaround
+            // needed.
 
             let mock = Arc::new(crate::wezterm::MockWezterm::new());
             mock.add_default_pane(42).await;
